content:
# Revelation Chapter 17 -- The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Codex Sinaiticus, pages 289-290 | **Source Image:** REV_017_page_001.jpg, REV_017_page_002.jpg

1. And one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls came and spoke with me, saying, "Come, I will show you the judgment of the great harlot who sits upon many waters,

2. with whom the kings of the earth committed fornication, and those who dwell upon the earth were made drunk with the wine of her fornication."

3. And he carried me away in the Spirit into a wilderness. And I saw a woman sitting upon a scarlet beast, full of names of blasphemy, having seven heads and ten horns.

4. And the woman was clothed in purple and scarlet, and adorned with gold and precious stone and pearls, having in her hand a golden cup full of abominations and the unclean things of her fornication,

5. and upon her forehead a name written, a mystery: BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF THE HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH.

6. And I saw the woman drunk with the blood of the saints and with the blood of the witnesses of Jesus. And when I saw her, I marveled with great amazement.

7. And the angel said to me, "Why did you marvel? I will tell you the mystery of the woman and of the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads and the ten horns.

8. The beast that you saw was, and is not, and is about to come up out of the abyss and go to destruction. And those who dwell upon the earth, whose name has not been written in the book of life from the foundation of the world, will marvel when they see the beast, that it was, and is not, and will be present.

9. Here is the mind that has wisdom: The seven heads are seven mountains upon which the woman sits,

10. and they are seven kings. The five have fallen, the one is, the other has not yet come; and when he comes, he must remain a little while.

11. And the beast that was and is not, it is itself also an eighth, and is of the seven, and goes to destruction.

12. And the ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they receive authority as kings for one hour with the beast.

13. These have one purpose, and they give their power and authority to the beast.

14. These will make war with the Lamb, and the Lamb will overcome them, because he is Lord of lords and King of kings -- and those with him are called and chosen and faithful."

15. And he says to me, "The waters that you saw, where the harlot sits, are peoples and multitudes and nations and tongues.

16. And the ten horns that you saw, and the beast, these will hate the harlot, and will make her desolate and naked, and will eat her flesh, and will burn her with fire.

17. For God put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose, and to act with one purpose, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God are fulfilled.

18. And the woman whom you saw is the great city that has dominion over the kings of the earth."

---
## SOPHIA's Council Notes

The following verses achieved substantial agreement across all three translators, requiring only minor stylistic harmonization:

- **17:3** -- All three translators agreed on the core rendering of the Spirit-wilderness transport and the description of the scarlet beast with blasphemous names, seven heads, and ten horns.
- **17:10** -- All three agreed on the essential structure: five fallen, one present, one yet to come who must remain a short time.
- **17:11** -- Universal agreement that the beast is an eighth king yet belongs to the seven, heading for destruction.
- **17:12** -- All three agreed the ten horns are ten kings receiving authority for one hour with the beast.
- **17:18** -- All three agreed that the woman is the great city ruling over earth's kings.

---
## Disagreement Log

**Verse 1:**
- EMET: "the judgment of the great harlot, the [one] sitting upon many waters"
- LOGOS: "the judgment of the great harlot who sits upon many waters"
- PNEUMA: "the sentencing of the great prostitute who sits enthroned over many waters"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "the judgment of the great harlot who sits upon many waters." The Greek *krima* is best rendered "judgment" (EMET/LOGOS) rather than "sentencing" (PNEUMA), as it encompasses the full judicial verdict. "Harlot" is preferred over "prostitute" for consistency with the Sinaiticus register. PNEUMA's "enthroned" is interpretive -- the Greek *kathemenes* means "sitting." LOGOS's smooth relative clause is adopted over EMET's bracketed literalism.

**Verse 2:**
- EMET: "the [ones] dwelling upon the earth were made drunk from the wine of the fornication of her"
- LOGOS: "those who dwell upon the earth were made drunk with the wine of her fornication"
- PNEUMA: "the inhabitants of the earth have been made drunk on the wine of her debauchery"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Fornication" is retained over PNEUMA's "debauchery" as closer to *porneia*. LOGOS's word order is more natural than EMET's genitive chain.

**Verse 4:**
- EMET: "having been gilded with gold and precious stone and pearls"
- LOGOS: "adorned with gold and precious stone and pearls"
- PNEUMA: "glittering with gold, precious stones, and pearls"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts "adorned" (LOGOS). The Greek *kechrysomene* specifically means "gilded/covered in gold" (EMET), but "adorned" captures the broader sense elegantly. PNEUMA's "glittering" is too interpretive, and pluralizes "stones" against the Sinaiticus singular.

**Verse 5:**
- EMET: "BABYLON THE GREAT, THE MOTHER OF THE HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH"
- LOGOS: "Babylon the Great, the Mother of the Harlots and of the Abominations of the Earth"
- PNEUMA: "BABYLON THE GREAT, MOTHER OF PROSTITUTES AND OF EVERY ABOMINATION ON EARTH"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses all-capitals (EMET/PNEUMA) as the inscription format, but follows EMET/LOGOS for "THE MOTHER OF THE HARLOTS AND OF THE ABOMINATIONS OF THE EARTH." PNEUMA's "PROSTITUTES" becomes "HARLOTS" for consistency, and "EVERY ABOMINATION ON EARTH" departs from the Greek genitive "of the abominations of the earth."

**Verse 6:**
- EMET: "being drunk from the blood of the holy [ones]... I marveled, having seen her, [with] great amazement"
- LOGOS: "drunk from the blood of the saints... I marveled with great wonder"
- PNEUMA: "intoxicated -- drunk on the blood of God's holy people... I was utterly astonished"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "drunk with the blood of the saints... I marveled with great amazement." "Saints" (LOGOS) is standard for *hagion*. "Amazement" (EMET) is preferred over "wonder" (LOGOS) or "astonished" (PNEUMA) as *thauma mega* is closer to "great amazement." PNEUMA's "Yeshua" (EMET) vs. "Jesus" (LOGOS/PNEUMA): SOPHIA uses "Jesus" as the standard English rendering.

**Verse 7:**
- EMET: "the [one] carrying her, the [one] having the seven heads"
- LOGOS: "the beast that carries her, which has the seven heads"
- PNEUMA: "the beast she rides, the one with seven heads"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "That carries her" is accurate to the Greek active participle. PNEUMA's "she rides" reverses the grammatical subject. EMET's bracketed articles are unnecessary.

**Verse 8:**
- EMET: "whose name has not been written upon the scroll of the life"
- LOGOS: "whose names have not been written in the book of life"
- PNEUMA: "whose names have not been written in the Book of Life"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "whose name has not been written in the book of life." Sinaiticus reads the singular *onoma* ("name"), as EMET correctly notes. "Book" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) is preferred over "scroll" for *biblion* in this fixed expression. The Sinaiticus variant "will be present" (*parestai*) is adopted unanimously.

**Verse 9:**
- EMET: "Here [is] the mind, the [one] having wisdom. The seven heads are seven mountains, where the woman sits upon them."
- LOGOS: "Here is the mind that has wisdom: the seven heads are seven mountains upon which the woman sits"
- PNEUMA: "This calls for a mind with wisdom: The seven heads represent seven hills on which the woman sits enthroned."
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS's construction. "Mountains" (EMET/LOGOS) is preferred over "hills" (PNEUMA) for *ore*. PNEUMA's "represent" and "enthroned" are interpretive additions.

**Verse 13:**
- EMET: "These have one mind, and the power and authority of them they give to the beast."
- LOGOS: "These have one purpose, and they give their power and authority to the beast."
- PNEUMA: "These kings are united in purpose and will hand over their power and authority to the beast."
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. The Greek *gnome* means "purpose/intent" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) rather than "mind" (EMET). PNEUMA adds "kings" and "united in" which are interpretive expansions.

**Verse 14:**
- EMET: "the Lamb will conquer them"
- LOGOS: "the Lamb will overcome them"
- PNEUMA: "the Lamb will triumph over them"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA uses "overcome" (LOGOS) for *nikesei* -- it is accurate and dignified without the military overtone of "conquer" (EMET) or the triumphalist expansion of "triumph over" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 15:**
- EMET: "The waters which you saw, where the harlot sits"
- LOGOS: "The waters that you saw, where the harlot sits"
- PNEUMA: "The waters you saw, where the prostitute sits enthroned"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "That" over "which" for the relative pronoun; "harlot" over "prostitute"; no "enthroned."

**Verse 16:**
- EMET: "having been made desolate they will make her, and naked, and the flesh of her they will eat, and her they will burn with fire"
- LOGOS: "will make her desolate and naked, and will eat her flesh, and will burn her with fire"
- PNEUMA: "will strip her bare, devour her flesh, and burn her with fire"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA follows LOGOS. EMET's hyper-literal word order is awkward. PNEUMA's "strip her bare" and "devour" are vivid but depart from the Greek terms. "Make her desolate and naked" preserves both Greek ideas faithfully.

**Verse 17:**
- EMET: "For the God gave into the hearts of them to do the purpose of him... until the words of the God will be completed"
- LOGOS: "For God put it into their hearts to do his purpose... until the words of God are fulfilled"
- PNEUMA: "For God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose... until God's words are fulfilled"
- **Resolution:** SOPHIA adopts a blend: "For God put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose, and to act with one purpose, and to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God are fulfilled." "Carry out" (PNEUMA) is slightly clearer than "do" (EMET/LOGOS). "Fulfilled" (LOGOS/PNEUMA) over "completed" (EMET) for *telesthesetai*.

-- SOPHIA
